bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My top values across all issues are valueD for everything, giving me utility of 0.1*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 1.0. There's no offer history to read, so I'll open strong with my ideal bid.

The opponent opened with their best offer (all valueA), which gives me minimum utility (0.2 across all issues = 0.2 total). We're very early (t=0.02), so I should stay near my top bid. I'll maintain my maximum utility bid since there's no reason to concede yet - the opponent is clearly starting from their ideal position just as I am.

The opponent is consistently offering all valueA (their best, my worst), showing no concession at all. We're very early (t=0.04), so there's no need to concede yet. I'll maintain my top-utility bid (all valueD) since it's still very early in the negotiation and the opponent hasn't moved at all. I'll stay firm to signal that I won't capitulate easily.

We're very early in the negotiation (t=0.06) and both sides are holding firm at their maximum utility bids. The opponent is consistently offering all valueA (which gives me minimum utility of 0.2 across all issues), while I've been offering all valueD. Since it's still very early, I should stay near my top utility but make a small concession to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll concede on issueA (my lowest weight issue at 0.1) by dropping to valueC, while holding firm on the more important issues.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating their maximum bid (all valueA) every turn, showing no concession at all. We're still very early (t=0.08), so there's no need to concede significantly. I'll hold near my top utility while making a small concession on issueA (my lowest-weight issue) to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll keep issueD, issueC, and issueB at valueD since those are my highest-weight issues.

The opponent has finally moved from their all-valueA position to a more interesting offer: issueA: valueC, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueC. This gives me utility of 0.1*0.73 + 0.2*0.47 + 0.3*1.00 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.073 + 0.094 + 0.30 + 0.292 = 0.759. That's a significant concession from them. We're still early (t=0.10), so I should stay near my top utility but make a small concession to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll concede on issueA (low weight) and issueB (medium weight) while holding firm on issueC and issueD (highest weights). I'll offer issueA: valueC, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD for utility = 0.1*0.73 + 0.2*0.73 + 0.3*1.00 + 0.4*1.00 = 0.073 + 0.146 + 0.30 + 0.40 = 0.919.

The opponent has been mostly holding at all-valueA but just made a more interesting offer at t=0.12: issueA: valueD, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueC. Their utility to me = 0.1*1.0 + 0.2*0.47 + 0.3*0.47 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.1 + 0.094 + 0.141 + 0.292 = 0.627. We're still early (t=0.12), so I should stay high but show some movement. My current bid at t=0.10 gives me utility = 0.1*0.73 + 0.2*0.73 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.073 + 0.146 + 0.3 + 0.4 = 0.919. I'll concede slightly on issueA (low weight) and issueB (medium weight) while holding firm on issueC and issueD (highest weights), targeting around 0.87 utility.

The opponent has been mostly offering all-valueA bids (their best), with one interesting deviation at t=0.10 where they offered {issueA: valueC, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueC} - which gave me utility of 0.1*0.73 + 0.2*0.47 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.073 + 0.094 + 0.3 + 0.292 = 0.759. Their latest bid at t=0.14 went back to mostly valueA with issueD: valueB, which is worse for me. We're still very early (t=0.14), so I should hold firm near my top utility. My current bid gives me utility of 0.1*0.73 + 0.2*0.73 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*1.0 = 0.073 + 0.146 + 0.3 + 0.4 = 0.919. I'll maintain a strong position but slightly vary to avoid exact repetition - keeping issueD and issueC at valueD (my highest weight issues) while making a small concession on issueA.

The opponent's offers are inconsistent and seem exploratory - they've been mostly offering all-A values but occasionally mixing in higher values. Their latest offer (t=0.16) gives me: issueA: valueC (0.1×0.73=0.073), issueB: valueD (0.2×1.0=0.2), issueC: valueA (0.3×0.2=0.06), issueD: valueC (0.4×0.73=0.292) = ~0.625 utility. We're still early (t=0.16), so I should maintain a high bid but make a small concession. My current bid at t=0.14 was ~0.88 utility. I'll concede slightly on issueA (lowest weight) while holding firm on issueD and issueC (highest weights), and slightly concede on issueB.

The opponent is still very early in the negotiation and their offers are inconsistent - they keep reverting to all-valueA bids which give me very low utility (0.2 across the board = 0.2 total). Their best offer to me was at t=0.10 (issueA: valueC, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueC) which gives me 0.1*0.73 + 0.2*0.47 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.073 + 0.094 + 0.3 + 0.292 = 0.759. We're only at t=0.18, so I should stay firm on my high-priority issues (issueD and issueC) while making small concessions on lower-priority ones. I'll keep issueD at valueD and issueC at valueD, and slightly concede on issueA and issueB to show movement.

The opponent is mostly holding firm on their preferred values (valueA for most issues, valueB for issueD), which gives me very low utility (~0.2 across the board). Their best offer to me was at t=0.10: {issueA: valueC, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueC}, which gives me 0.1*0.73 + 0.2*0.47 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.073 + 0.094 + 0.3 + 0.292 = 0.759. We're still early (t=0.2), so I should continue holding relatively firm on my high-weight issues (issueD, issueC) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. I'll concede issueA to valueA (lowest weight) and keep issueB at valueC, issueC at valueD, issueD at valueD to signal willingness to move slightly.

The opponent is mostly stuck on valueA for issueA, issueB, issueC, and valueB for issueD. Their best offer to me was at t=0.10: {issueA: valueC, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueC} which gives me utility of 0.1*0.73 + 0.2*0.47 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.073 + 0.094 + 0.3 + 0.292 = 0.759. We're still early (t=0.22), so I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueD, issueC) while making small concessions on low-weight issues. I'll keep issueC and issueD at valueD (my top values for the most important issues) and concede issueA to valueA and issueB to valueB to signal movement.

The opponent keeps returning to {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueB} repeatedly, which gives me utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*0.2 + 0.4*0.47 = 0.02+0.04+0.06+0.188 = 0.308. We're still early (t=0.24), so I shouldn't accept that yet. My most recent bid was {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueB, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD} with utility ~0.1*0.2+0.2*0.47+0.3*1.0+0.4*1.0 = 0.02+0.094+0.3+0.4 = 0.814. The opponent seems to care most about issueD (they keep offering valueB there) and issueC (always valueA). I should concede on issueA (low weight for me) and try to move issueD slightly while keeping issueC and issueD high for me. Let me try conceding issueD to valueC (still good for me: 0.73) while keeping issueC at valueD, and offer issueB at valueB to signal movement.

The opponent's latest offer (t=0.26) is {issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueA}, which gives me utility: 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.3*0.47 + 0.4*0.20 = 0.02 + 0.04 + 0.141 + 0.08 = 0.281 — too low. The opponent seems to be holding mostly at their preferred values (all A's) with occasional small concessions. We're still early (t=0.26), so I don't need to rush. My highest-weight issue is issueD (0.4), so I should hold firm there. I'll concede on issueA (lowest weight) and issueB, while keeping issueC and issueD high. My last bid was {A:valueA, B:valueB, C:valueD, D:valueC} with utility 0.1*0.20 + 0.2*0.47 + 0.3*1.00 + 0.4*0.73 = 0.02+0.094+0.30+0.292 = 0.706. I'll try moving issueC slightly to valueC to signal willingness to compromise while keeping issueD at valueC or valueD.

The opponent seems to be oscillating and not conceding much - they keep returning to low values especially on issueD (valueA) which is my most important issue. Their last offer gives me utility: issueA(0.2×0.1) + issueB(0.2×0.2) + issueC(0.47×0.3) + issueD(0.2×0.4) = 0.02+0.04+0.141+0.08 = 0.281. I need to find a compromise. At t=0.28 we're still early-mid game, so I can hold firm on issueD (my highest weight) while conceding more on issueA and issueB. I'll try issueD: valueD, issueC: valueC, issueB: valueB, issueA: valueA to signal flexibility on lower-weight issues while protecting my top priority.
